page.content: The table presents a comprehensive analysis of various inflammatory markers and fatty acid profiles in relation to hepatitis E virus (HEV) infection, specifically focusing on data collected at 28 days post-infection (dpi). The metrics of interest include C-reactive protein (CRP) and interleukin-6 (IL-6), both of which are pivotal indicators of inflammatory response. The correlation coefficients indicated for these markers suggest a significant relationship with the presence of HEV RNA, as evidenced by the positive-strand and negative-strand HEV RNA detection columns. Furthermore, the fatty acid composition, including omega-3 (n-3) and omega-6 (n-6) fatty acids, provides insight into the potential modulatory effects of dietary components on inflammatory pathways during viral infections.

page.content: The experimental groups analyzed in this study indicate varying levels of inflammation and immune response, which may have implications for therapeutic strategies targeting HEV. Overall, this table underscores the complex interplay between inflammatory markers, fatty acid profiles, and viral load, contributing to our understanding of HEV pathophysiology.
